g.text: Plans, then EXECUTES the runbook via CMP-DEPLOY-EXEC.
Now runs az / terraform / docker (the old "never runs
az / docker / brew" non-goal is retired).
Manages auth, dry-runs before apply, pauses only at
irreversible steps + go-live, halts on first error.
Agent-driven Bash — NOT a new mdd CLI verb. Outside the
parity gate. Realizes USE-DEPLOY-EXECUTE; go-live gated
parity, no @sec parity. A deployment has no code-derived
counterpart, so by design it is not routed into the gate
— executing it instead of guiding it does not change that.
read runbook.md as the execution plan
auth, apply, provision secrets, migrate, route traffic
auth handshake + confirm irreversible steps & go-live
